mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2016-09-15 indicates near-zero rates
regime_hint: monetary easing or zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive FEDFUNDS momentum does not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative momentum (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals strengthening labor market and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings. Month-over-month growth in PAYEMS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations upward.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with sustained job growth
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS momentum fails to produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) measure forward-looking construction demand and household formation expectations. When HOUST rises above its 12-month moving average, it signals confidence in residential investment and broader economic expansion, predicting positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 479.0 thousand units as of 2016-08-15
failure_condition: HOUST above 12-month MA does not produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS and HOUST accelerate simultaneously (month-over-month growth > 0.5%), the combined signal indicates broad-based demand expansion that drives equity valuations upward over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand breadth that neither series alone captures.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% AND HOUST MoM growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389,320 million dollars as of 2016-09-15
fact:HOUST at 479.0 thousand units as of 2016-08-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand acceleration across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST acceleration does not produce positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real interest rates and policy stance. When CPI growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, the Fed faces a policy dilemma that creates uncertainty. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice tail risk of policy error.
regime_hint: policy constraint regime with rising inflation at zero lower bound
failure_condition: Inflation-policy constraint does not produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a lagging indicator of labor market slack. When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE deterioration does not produce negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic expansion. Sustained quarterly growth above 2.0% annualized signals healthy demand and earnings growth. When GDPC1 growth accelerates quarter-over-quarter, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days are positive as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,380.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-04-15
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% does not produce positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.08 over any 2-year rolling window.
